question: If 8 – 2 = 10 and 7 – 3 = 13, then 6 – 4 = ?

7 – 3 = (7 − 3) + 3² = 4 + 9 = 13 ✔️

Now,
6 – 4 = (6 − 4) + 4² = 2 + 16 = 18

question: At what time (approximately) between 8 AM and 9 AM will the hour and minute hands of the clock be opposite to each other?

option_2: 8:11
option_3: 8:15
option_4: 8:20
solution_text:
Opposite ⇒ Angle = 180°

Formula:
30H − 5.5M = ±180

Case (+180):
30×8 − 5.5M = 180
240 − 5.5M = 180
5.5M = 60
M = 60 / 5.5 = 10.9 min ≈ 11 min

Case (−180):
30×8 − 5.5M = −180
240 − 5.5M = −180
5.5M = 420
M = 420 / 5.5 = 76.36 min (invalid > 60)

Final Answer:

Valid time = 8:11 AM

question:
In the year 2017, which of the following months had 5 Sundays?
I. January
II. October
option_1: Only I
option_2: Only II
option_3: Both I and II
option_4: Neither I nor II
solution_text:
A month has 5 Sundays if:

  • It has 31 days, and
  • The first Sunday falls on 1st, 2nd, or 3rd date

1 January 2017

Day = (Date + Month Code + Century Code + Last 2 digits of year + (Last 2 digits ÷ 4)) mod 7

Day = 1 + 0 + 6 + 17 + 4 = 28

Now,
28 mod 7 = 0 = Sunday

Check January

  • January has 31 days
  • 1 Jan = Sunday

So Sundays fall on:
1, 8, 15, 22, 29 → 5 Sundays

✔ January is correct

Check October

Total odd days from Jan to Sept:

  • Jan (3) + Feb (0) + Mar (3) + Apr (2) + May (3) + Jun (2) + Jul (3) + Aug (3) + Sep (2)
    = 21 → 0 odd day

👉 So 1 Oct 2017 = Sunday

  • 1 October 2017 was also a Sunday
  • October has 31 days

So Sundays fall on:
1, 8, 15, 22, 29 → 5 Sundays

✔ October is correct.

question:

solution_text:
Explanation:

    • “The car is fast” → ki lo va po
    • “The car is slow” → ki lo to po

  • Compare 1st and 3rd sentences:
    • “The car is fast” → ki lo va po
    • “The bike is fast” → do lo va po
    • “The is fast” is common, so the remaining codes ki / do correspond to car / bike.
  • Compare all three sentences:
    • “The is” is common, so lo po correspond to “The is”.
  • Using the above assignments:

    • bike → do
    • fast → va

✅ Code for “car bike fast” = ki do va

question:
Regarding the 'Misl' system in Sikh history, which among the following statements is/are correct?

1- The Misls were democratic confederacies that emerged after the decline of the Mughal Empire.

2- Maharaja Ranjit Singh belonged to the Sukerchakia Misl.
option_1: 1 only
option_2: 2 only
option_3: Both 1 & 2
option_4: Neither 1 & 2
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Both 1 & 2

Explanation

  • Statement 1: The Misls were democratic confederacies that emerged after the decline of the Mughal Empire. ✔ Correct — The Misl system in Sikh history consisted of 12 confederacies (Misls) formed during the 18th century. Each Misl was a democratic military brotherhood led by a chief, and decisions were often taken collectively. They arose as the Mughal power weakened in Punjab.
  • Statement 2: Maharaja Ranjit Singh belonged to the Sukerchakia Misl. ✔ Correct — Maharaja Ranjit Singh, who later unified the Misls and established the Sikh Empire, was from the Sukerchakia Misl. His leadership transformed the fragmented confederacies into a strong centralized kingdom.

Thus, both statements are correct.

option_3: He decides whether a particular bill is a Money Bill or otherwise.
option_4: He presides over the joint sittings of both the Houses of Parliament.
solution_text:
The incorrect statement is: He is appointed by the President from the majority party members.

✅ Explanation

  • He remains in office even after the dissolution of the Lok Sabha → ✅ Correct. The Speaker continues until a new Speaker is elected.
  • He presides over the joint sittings of both the Houses of Parliament → ✅ Correct. The Speaker of Lok Sabha chairs joint sessions.
  • He decides whether a particular bill is a Money Bill or otherwise → ✅ Correct. This is one of the Speaker’s exclusive powers.
  • He is appointed by the President from the majority party members → ❌ Incorrect. The Speaker is elected by the members of the Lok Sabha from among themselves, not appointed by the President.

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the planet Jupiter in the solar system and answer which of them is/are correct?

1- It has the shortest day duration among all the planets in our system.

2- It possesses a prominent system of rings made mostly of frozen water ice.
option_1: 1 only
option_2: 2 only
option_3: Both 1 & 2
option_4: Neither 1 & 2
solution_text:
The correct answer is: 1 only. Jupiter indeed has the shortest day among all planets, but its ring system is faint and made of dust, not frozen water ice.

✅Explanation:

  1. Statement 1: It has the shortest day duration among all the planets in our system.
    • True. Jupiter rotates once on its axis in about 9.9 hours, making it the planet with the shortest day in the solar system.
  2. Statement 2: It possesses a prominent system of rings made mostly of frozen water ice.
    • False. Jupiter does have rings, but they are very faint and composed mainly of dust particles, not frozen water ice. Saturn’s rings, by contrast, are prominent and made largely of water ice.

question: Which of the following is NOT a part of the Directive Principles of State Policy?
option_1: Protection and improvement of environment and safeguarding of forests.
option_2: Organization of village panchayats for the purpose of local governance.
option_3: Separation of the executive from the legislature at the central level.
option_4: Provision of early childhood care and education for children under six.
solution_text:
The incorrect statement is: Separation of the executive from the legislature at the central level.

✅ Explanation

  • Protection and improvement of environment and safeguarding of forests → ✅ Correct. Added by the 42nd Amendment (1976) as a Directive Principle.
  • Organization of village panchayats for the purpose of local governance → ✅ Correct. Directive Principle under Article 40.
  • Provision of early childhood care and education for children under six → ✅ Correct. Directive Principle under Article 45 (after 86th Amendment, 2002).
  • Separation of the executive from the legislature at the central level → ❌ Incorrect. The Directive Principles mention separation of judiciary from the executive (Article 50), not separation of executive from legislature.

question: With reference to the Indian National Congress sessions, the session that passed the ‘Purna Swaraj’ resolution and declared 26 January 1930 as Independence Day was held at:
option_1: Lahore
option_2: Karachi
option_3: Lucknow
option_4: Calcutta
solution_text:
The session that passed the ‘Purna Swaraj’ resolution and declared 26 January 1930 as Independence Day was held at Lahore.

✅Explanation:

  • Indian National Congress Session: Lahore, December 1929.
  • President of the Session: Jawaharlal Nehru.
  • Key Resolution:
    • Adoption of the ‘Purna Swaraj’ (Complete Independence) resolution.
    • Declared 26 January 1930 as Independence Day, to be celebrated across the country.
  • Significance:
    • Marked a shift from demanding dominion status to complete independence.
    • Led to the Civil Disobedience Movement and the Salt March in 1930.

question: The HOPE mission (2025) conducted by ISRO in Ladakh is primarily related to:
option_1: Testing space‑docking with a foreign satellite
option_2: High‑altitude isolation to study human endurance
option_3: Simulating a Mars‑landing in a lunar‑like terrain
option_4: Launch of a small‑satellite cluster for quantum communication
solution_text:
The HOPE mission (2025) conducted by ISRO in Ladakh is primarily related to simulating lunar and Martian conditions in a high-altitude environment to study human endurance and challenges of long-duration space missions. It is part of India’s human spaceflight program, supporting future crewed missions to the Moon and Mars.

✅ Key Facts about HOPE Mission

  • Full Form: Himalayan Outpost for Planetary Exploration (HOPE).
  • Location: Tso Kar Valley, Ladakh (chosen for its extreme, barren, high-altitude terrain resembling lunar and Martian landscapes).
  • Conducted by: ISRO, with support from Protoplanet (a Bengaluru-based space outreach organization).
  • Year: 2025.
  • Objective:
    • Simulate space-like conditions on Earth.
    • Study physiological, psychological, and operational challenges faced by humans in isolation and extreme environments.
    • Test technologies and health protocols for future interplanetary missions.
  • Long-term Goal: To prepare for India’s crewed lunar mission by 2040 and eventual Mars exploration.

question: Arrange the following Himalayan ranges from the North to the South direction:
option_1: Shiwalik, Pir Panjal, Zaskar, Ladakh, and the Karakoram range.
option_2: Karakoram, Zaskar, Ladakh, Shiwalik, and the Pir Panjal range.
option_3: Ladakh, Karakoram, Pir Panjal, Zaskar, and the Shiwalik range.
option_4: Karakoram, Ladakh, Zaskar, Pir Panjal, and the Shiwalik range.
solution_text:
The correct arrangement from North to South is: Karakoram → Ladakh → Zaskar → Pir Panjal → Shiwalik

✅ Explanation

  • Karakoram Range → Northernmost, lies beyond the Himalayas, includes K2.
  • Ladakh Range → South of Karakoram, between Indus and Shyok rivers.
  • Zaskar Range → Lies south of Ladakh, parallel to the main Himalayas.
  • Pir Panjal Range → Lies further south, part of the Lesser Himalayas, in Jammu & Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh.
  • Shiwalik Range → Southernmost, also called Outer Himalayas, closest to the plains.

question: The 'Amrit Bharat Station Scheme' is primarily related to the:
option_1: Temple Architecture
option_2: Railway Infrastructure
option_3: Rural Electrification
option_4: Space Exploration
solution_text:
The Amrit Bharat Station Scheme is primarily related to: Railway Infrastructure.

✅ Explanation

  • Launched by the Ministry of Railways, the scheme aims to modernize and redevelop 1,275 railway stations across India.
  • Focus areas:
    • Better passenger amenities (waiting halls, toilets, lifts, escalators).
    • Improved accessibility (divyang-friendly features).
    • Modern design with upgraded platforms, roofing, and lighting.
    • Integration with local culture and heritage in station architecture.
  • Objective: Enhance ease of travel, improve passenger experience, and make stations world-class hubs.

question: Which of the following Indian states does NOT share a border with Bhutan?
option_1: Sikkim
option_2: West Bengal
option_3: Meghalaya
option_4: Arunachal Pradesh
solution_text:
The Indian state that does NOT share a border with Bhutan is: Meghalaya.

✅ Explanation

  • Bhutan’s neighboring Indian states:
    • Sikkim (to the west)
    • West Bengal (south)
    • Assam (south-east)
    • Arunachal Pradesh (east)
  • Meghalaya lies further south of Assam and does not touch Bhutan’s boundary.

question: Which of the following is NOT a part of the 'Revenue Expenditure'?
option_1: Repayment of loans
option_2: Interest payments
option_3: Salary of staff
option_4: Subsidies given
solution_text:
The item that is NOT a part of Revenue Expenditure is: Repayment of loans.

✅ Explanation

  • Revenue Expenditure → Day-to-day expenses of the government that do not create assets or reduce liabilities. Examples:
    • Interest payments
    • Salaries of staff
    • Subsidies
  • Capital Expenditure → Expenses that create assets or reduce liabilities.
    • Repayment of loans falls under capital expenditure, because it reduces government liabilities.

question: The 'Suspensive Veto' of the President of India is used when he:
option_1: Rejects a bill passed by the Parliament and refuses to sign it.
option_2: Keeps a bill pending on his desk for an indefinite period of time.
option_3: Returns a bill to the Parliament for its further reconsideration.
option_4: Refers a bill to the Supreme Court for its opinion on legality.
solution_text:
The Suspensive Veto of the President of India is used when he: Returns a bill to the Parliament for its further reconsideration.

✅ Explanation

  • The President of India has three types of veto powers:
    1. Absolute Veto → Rejects a bill completely.
    2. Suspensive Veto → Returns a bill (except money bills) to Parliament for reconsideration. If Parliament passes it again (with or without amendments), the President must give assent.
    3. Pocket Veto → Keeps a bill pending indefinitely without taking any action.
  • Suspensive Veto is thus a delaying power, not a permanent rejection.

question: With which instrument is the legend Pt. Pannalal Ghosh associated?
option_1: Sitar
option_2: Bansuri
option_3: Sarod
option_4: Santoor
solution_text:
Pt. Pannalal Ghosh is associated with the Bansuri (flute).

✅ Explanation

  • He was a legendary Indian classical musician credited with introducing the bansuri (bamboo flute) into Hindustani classical music as a concert instrument.
  • Before him, the bansuri was mainly used in folk traditions and light music.
  • He developed the longer, larger bansuri capable of producing deeper, more expressive notes suitable for classical ragas.
  • His contribution elevated the bansuri to the status of a major classical instrument.

❌ Why Other Options Are Wrong

  • Sitar → Associated with maestros like Pt. Ravi Shankar.
  • Sarod → Associated with Ustad Amjad Ali Khan.
  • Santoor→ Associated with Pt. Shivkumar Sharma.

question: In the context of share markets, 'Bull' and 'Bear' are terms for:
option_1: Market Speculators
option_2: Stock Exchanges
option_3: Listed Companies
option_4: Banking Licenses
solution_text:
The terms ‘Bull’ and ‘Bear’ in the context of share markets refer to: Market Speculators.

✅ Explanation

  • Bull → A speculator who expects prices to rise and buys shares in anticipation of selling them later at a profit.
  • Bear → A speculator who expects prices to fall and sells shares (often without owning them, i.e., short selling) in anticipation of buying them back at a lower price.

These terms describe market sentiment and trading behavior, not institutions or licenses.

❌ Why Other Options Are Wrong

  • Stock Exchanges → Institutions like NSE, BSE; not referred to as bulls or bears.
  • Listed Companies → Firms whose shares are traded; not bulls or bears.
  • Banking Licenses → Regulatory permissions; unrelated to these terms.

question: Under Article 368, the Parliament can amend the Constitution by:
option_1: A simple majority of the members present and voting in each House.
option_2: A special majority and ratification by half of the state legislatures.
option_3: A special majority of the total membership of the House of People.
option_4: The prior permission and recommendation of the Supreme Court judges.
solution_text:
The correct answer is: A special majority and ratification by half of the state legislatures.

✅ Explanation

Under Article 368 of the Indian Constitution, the procedure for amendment depends on the type of provision being amended:

  1. By a simple majority → Some provisions can be amended by a simple majority (e.g., formation of new states, language provisions). These are not considered constitutional amendments under Article 368.
  2. By a special majority → Most provisions require a special majority (i.e., majority of the total membership of each House + two-thirds of members present and voting).
  3. By a special majority + ratification by half of the state legislatures → Certain provisions (like the election of the President, distribution of legislative powers, representation of states in Parliament, etc.) require not only a special majority in Parliament but also ratification by at least half of the state legislatures.
  4. No role of Supreme Court judges → The Supreme Court’s prior permission or recommendation is not required for constitutional amendments.

question: In the context of banking, the 'Statutory Liquidity Ratio' (SLR) is:
option_1: The cash that the commercial banks must keep with the Reserve Bank.
option_2: The rate of interest provided by the banks to the long-term depositors.
option_3: The rate at which the Reserve Bank lends money to the commercial banks.
option_4: The liquid assets banks must maintain with themselves as a percentage.
solution_text:
The Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) in banking refers to: The liquid assets banks must maintain with themselves as a percentage.

✅ Explanation

  • SLR is the minimum percentage of net demand and time liabilities (NDTL) that commercial banks are required to maintain in the form of liquid assets before offering credit.
  • These liquid assets can include:
    • Cash
    • Gold
    • Approved government securities (like bonds).
  • It is mandated by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) under the Banking Regulation Act.
  • Purpose:
    • To ensure financial stability.
    • To control inflation and regulate credit flow in the economy.

question: Which of the following is a 'Double Membrane' bound cell organelle?
option_1: Ribosome
option_2: Mitochondria
option_3: Lysosome
option_4: Centrosome
solution_text:
The double membrane bound cell organelle among the given options is: Mitochondria.

✅ Explanation

  • Mitochondria → Surrounded by two membranes:
    • Outer membrane → Smooth, acts as a boundary.
    • Inner membrane → Folded into cristae, increases surface area for ATP production.
    • Function: Known as the powerhouse of the cell, responsible for energy (ATP) generation.
  • Other options:
    • Ribosome → Not membrane-bound at all (made of rRNA and proteins).
    • Lysosome → Single membrane-bound organelle containing digestive enzymes.
    • Centrosome → non-membranous structure, involved in cell division (contains centrioles).

question: Which river originates from the 'Rakshas Tal' near the Mansarovar Lake?
option_1: Brahmaputra
option_2: Jhelum
option_3: Indus
option_4: Sutlej
solution_text:
The river that originates from Rakshas Tal near Mansarovar Lake is the: Sutlej River.

✅ Explanation

  • Rakshas Tal (Ravan Tal) and Mansarovar Lake are located in Tibet, near Mount Kailash.
  • The Sutlej River originates from Rakshas Tal, flows through Tibet, enters India in Himachal Pradesh, and eventually joins the Indus River in Pakistan.
  • Other rivers:
    • Brahmaputra → Originates from Angsi Glacier near Kailash, not Rakshas Tal.
    • Jhelum → Originates from Verinag spring in Jammu & Kashmir.
    • Indus → Originates from Sengge Zangbo (near Mount Kailash), not Rakshas Tal.

question: The 'Pattachitra' style of painting is a traditional art form of:
option_1: Odisha
option_2: Bihar
option_3: Kerala
option_4: Gujarat
solution_text:
The Pattachitra style of painting is a traditional art form of: Odisha.

✅ Explanation

  • Pattachitra literally means “picture on cloth” (Patta = cloth, Chitra = picture).
  • It is one of the oldest and most popular art forms of Odisha, dating back to ancient times.
  • Themes: Mostly mythological, especially stories of Lord Jagannath, Krishna, and Vishnu.
  • Characteristics:
    • Intricate details.
    • Rich colors (natural dyes).
    • Depiction of religious motifs.
    • Painted on cloth, dried palm leaves, or walls.

question: The 'Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita' (BNS), which became the focus of 2025 revisions, replaces:
option_1: The Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC)
option_2: The Indian Evidence Act of 1872
option_3: The Civil Procedure Code (CPC) of 1908
option_4: The Indian Penal Code (IPC) of 1860
solution_text:
The Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), which came into effect with the 2025 revisions, replaces the Indian Penal Code (IPC) of 1860.

✅ Explanation

  • Indian Penal Code (IPC), 1860 → The colonial-era criminal law framework that governed substantive criminal law in India.
  • Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023 (implemented in 2025) → Introduced as part of a major overhaul of India’s criminal justice system, replacing the IPC.
  • Alongside BNS, two other new codes were introduced:
    • Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) → Replaces the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC).
    • Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam (BSA) → Replaces the Indian Evidence Act of 1872.

question:
Regarding the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG), which among the following statements is/are correct?

1- He is appointed by the President for a term of six years or age 62.

2- He can be removed from his office only on the grounds of misbehaviour.
option_1: 1 only
option_2: 2 only
option_3: Both 1 & 2
option_4: Neither 1 & 2
solution_text:
Neither statement 1 nor 2 is correct regarding the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India. The CAG is appointed by the President for a term of six years or until age 65 (not 62), and removal requires the same process as for a Supreme Court judge, which includes proven misbehavior or incapacity along with a special majority in Parliament.

Correct Option

Neither 1 & 2

Statement Analysis

  • Statement 1: Incorrect—the term ends at age 65 or six years, whichever is earlier, as per Article 148 and the CAG's Act.
  • Statement 2: Incorrect—removal follows the full Supreme Court judge procedure (Parliamentary resolution with two-thirds majority for misbehavior or incapacity), not solely "misbehaviour."

question: The 'Escape Velocity' from the surface of the Earth is about:
option_1: 9.8 kilometers per second
option_2: 42.1 kilometers per second
option_3: 11.2 kilometers per second
option_4: 7.91 kilometers per second
solution_text:
The escape velocity from the surface of the Earth is about: 11.2 kilometers per second.

✅ Explanation

  • Escape velocity is the minimum speed required for an object to break free from Earth’s gravitational pull without further propulsion.
  • Formula:

where = gravitational constant, = mass of Earth, = radius of Earth.

  • For Earth, this comes out to approximately 11.2 km/s.

question: The 'Income Tax Act, 2025', which replaces the 1961 Act, primarily aims to:
option_1: Increase the corporate tax rate for all the small-scale industries.
option_2: Consolidate and simplify the tax laws to enhance ease of compliance.
option_3: Abolish the concept of capital gains tax for the individual earners.
option_4: Nationalize all the private banking institutions within the country.
solution_text:
The correct answer is: Consolidate and simplify the tax laws to enhance ease of compliance.

✅ Explanation

  • The Income Tax Act, 1961 was the long-standing framework for direct taxation in India.
  • The Income Tax Act, 2025 has been introduced to replace the 1961 Act with the primary aim of:
    • Simplifying and consolidating tax provisions that had become complex due to numerous amendments over decades.
    • Enhancing ease of compliance for taxpayers by reducing ambiguity and streamlining procedures.
    • Modernizing tax administration in line with digital governance and transparency.

question: Regarding the 'Standard Meridian of India', which of the following is correct?
option_1: It passes through the city of Mirzapur and is at 82°60' East longitude.
option_2: It passes through the city of Mirzapur and is at 82°30' West longitude.
option_3: It passes through the city of Mirzapur and is at 82°30' East longitude.
option_4: It passes through the city of Mirzapur and is at 82°60' West longitude.
solution_text:
The correct statement is: It passes through the city of Mirzapur and is at 82°30' East longitude.

✅ Explanation

  • The Standard Meridian of India is the longitude chosen to serve as the reference for Indian Standard Time (IST).
  • It is located at 82°30' East longitude, which passes near Mirzapur in Uttar Pradesh.
  • This meridian was selected because it lies roughly in the middle of India’s east–west extent, ensuring a uniform time zone across the country.

question: The 'Satyashodhak Samaj' was founded in the year 1873 with the primary aim of:
option_1: Promoting the education of women and the lower caste individuals.
option_2: Reforming the orthodox practices within the Brahmo Samaj movement.
option_3: Establishing a separate political party for the Marathi speaking people.
option_4: Translating ancient Sanskrit texts into the common Marathi language.
solution_text:
The 'Satyashodhak Samaj' was founded in 1873 by Jyotirao Phule with the primary aim of promoting equality and education for women and lower-caste individuals, challenging caste-based discrimination and Brahmanical dominance.

✅ Correct Answer

Option: Promoting the education of women and the lower caste individuals.

Detailed Explanation:

  • Founder: Jyotirao Phule (with active support from his wife Savitribai Phule).
  • Year: 24 September 1873, Pune (then Poona), Maharashtra.
  • Aim:
    • To provide education and social rights to marginalized communities, especially Shudras, Ati-Shudras (Dalits), women, and peasants.
    • To challenge Brahmanical dominance and orthodox caste practices.
    • To promote social justice, equality, and upliftment of oppressed groups.
  • Impact:
    • Inspired later Dalit movements and social reform initiatives.
    • Savitribai Phule led the women’s wing, emphasizing female education and empowerment.

question:
Read the following passage carefully and answer the given questions:

The rise of "Synthetic Media" has fundamentally altered the landscape of digital authenticity, blurring the lines between verified reality and algorithmic fabrication. While generative AI offers unprecedented creative tools for filmmakers and educators, it simultaneously equips malicious actors with the ability to engineer "deepfakes" that can destabilize financial markets or influence geopolitical discourse. The speed at which these models iterate far outpaces the development of detection frameworks, leaving a "verification gap" that threatens the core of public trust in visual evidence.

In response, a paradigm shift toward "Content Provenance" is emerging as the primary defense mechanism against digital deception. Unlike traditional reactive moderation, which attempts to identify falsehoods after they go viral, provenance focuses on cryptographically marking the origin of a media file at the moment of its creation. By embedding metadata that tracks the lineage of an image or video, stakeholders aim to create a transparent digital ecosystem. However, the success of this approach hinges on global adoption by hardware manufacturers and software developers alike, a feat yet to be fully realized.

Q- Which of the following best describes the "verification gap" mentioned in the first stanza?
option_1: The technical delay in rendering high-resolution AI-generated media.
option_2: The disparity between AI generation speed and detection capabilities.
option_3: The economic distance between AI developers and the general public.
option_4: The ethical conflict between creative freedom and digital security.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: The disparity between AI generation speed and detection capabilities

Explanation

  • The passage highlights that generative AI models produce synthetic media (like deepfakes) extremely quickly, while detection frameworks lag behind.
  • This mismatch creates a “verification gap”, meaning there is a delay or insufficiency in verifying authenticity compared to the speed of AI-generated content.
  • As a result, malicious content can spread before detection tools catch up, undermining public trust in digital evidence.

option_4: It focuses on the source and history rather than reacting to content.

question: Choose the correct spelling for the word meaning "a small, exclusive group".
option_1: Coterie
option_2: Cotarie

question:

solution_text:

question:
Change the voice of the following sentence:

One should always maintain a proper balance between professional commitments and personal well-being to lead a fulfilling life.
option_1: A proper balance ought to be maintained between professional commitments and personal well-being to lead a fulfilling life.
option_2: A proper balance is always maintained between professional commitments and personal well-being to lead a fulfilling life.
option_3: A proper balance must always be maintained between professional commitments and personal well-being to lead a fulfilling life.
option_4: A proper balance should always be maintained between professional commitments and personal well-being to lead a fulfilling life.
solution_text:
Answer: 4

Solution:

  • The sentence uses modal “should” (advisory tone): “One should always maintain…”.
  • Passive rule with modal: Object + modal + be + V3.
  • “Maintain” → “be maintained” in passive form.
  • Option 4 correctly follows: “should + be + maintained” and keeps the same meaning.
  • Option 1 uses “ought to”, which slightly changes the modal and is not exact.
  • Option 2 uses present tense (is maintained), losing the modal meaning.
  • Option 3 uses “must”, which changes the intensity (strong obligation).

Correct Sentence:
A proper balance should always be maintained between professional commitments and personal well-being to lead a fulfilling life.

question:
Change the narration of the following sentence:

He said to me, "I shall return your book as soon as I have finished reading it, which will probably be by next Sunday."
option_1: He told me that he would return my book as soon as he has finished reading it, which will probably be by the following Sunday.
option_2: He told me that he would return my book as soon as he had finished reading it, which would probably be by the following Sunday.
option_3: He told me that he would return my book as soon as he finished reading it, which would probably be by the following Sunday.
option_4: He told me that he shall return my book as soon as he had finished reading it, which would probably be by the following Sunday.
solution_text:
Answer: 2

Solution:

  • The reporting verb is in past tense (“said”), so tense backshift is required.
  • “shall return” → “would return” (future → conditional).
  • “have finished” (present perfect) → “had finished” (past perfect).
  • “will probably be” → “would probably be” (future → conditional).
  • Time expression: “next Sunday” → “the following Sunday”.
  • Pronoun change: “your” → “my”, “I” → “he”.
  • Option 2 correctly follows all rules of tense, pronoun, and time change.
  • Option 1 uses “has finished” → incorrect tense.
  • Option 3 uses “finished” instead of “had finished” → incorrect backshift.
  • Option 4 keeps “shall return” → incorrect (no backshift).

Correct Sentence:
He told me that he would return my book as soon as he had finished reading it, which would probably be by the following Sunday.

question:

question:
Change the narration of the following sentence:

The teacher said to the boy, "Why have you not completed the assignment that I gave you in the previous class?"
option_1: The teacher asked the boy why had he not completed the assignment that she had given him in the previous class.
option_2: The teacher asked the boy why he has not completed the assignment that she had given him in the previous class.
option_3: The teacher asked the boy why he had not completed the assignment that she had given him in the previous class.
option_4: The teacher asked the boy why he had not completed the assignment that she gave him in the previous class.
solution_text:
Answer: 3

Solution:

  • The given sentence is an interrogative (question), so in indirect speech we use “asked” and remove the question form.
  • Rule: Why + subject + verb (no inversion like “had he”).
  • “have you not completed” (present perfect) → “had not completed” (past perfect) due to backshift.
  • Pronoun change: “you” → “he”, “I” → “she”, “you” → “him”.
  • Option 3 correctly follows tense change, structure, and pronoun rules.
  • Option 1 has wrong structure (why had he → incorrect inversion).
  • Option 2 keeps present perfect (has not completed) → incorrect tense.
  • Option 4 uses “gave” instead of “had given” → incorrect backshift.

Correct Sentence:
The teacher asked the boy why he had not completed the assignment that she had given him in the previous class.

question:
Identify the TYPE OF SENTENCE given below:

"Although the rain was heavy, the match continued until the final whistle blew."
option_1: Simple Sentence
option_2: Compound Sentence
option_3: Complex Sentence
option_4: Compound-Complex Sentence
solution_text:
Answer: 3

Solution:

  • The sentence contains a subordinate clause: “Although the rain was heavy” (dependent clause).
  • It also has a main clause: “the match continued until the final whistle blew”.
  • A sentence with one independent clause + at least one dependent clause is called a Complex Sentence.
  • Option 1 (Simple Sentence) → only one clause → incorrect.
  • Option 2 (Compound Sentence) → two independent clauses → incorrect.
  • Option 4 (Compound-Complex) → multiple independent + dependent clauses → not present here.

question:
Change the voice of the following sentence:

The regional manager instructed the subordinates to submit the detailed expenditure reports before the commencement of the meeting.
option_1: The subordinates were being instructed by the regional manager to submit the detailed expenditure reports before the meeting commenced.
option_2: The subordinates had been instructed by the regional manager to submit the detailed expenditure reports before the meeting commenced.
option_3: The subordinates are instructed by the regional manager to submit the detailed expenditure reports before the meeting commenced.
option_4: The subordinates were instructed by the regional manager to submit the detailed expenditure reports before the meeting commenced.
solution_text:
Answer: 4

Solution:

  • The given sentence is in Simple Past Tense (Active Voice): “instructed”.
  • Passive rule: Object + was/were + V3 + by + Subject.
  • Here, “the subordinates” becomes the subject → “were instructed” is used.
  • Option 4 correctly maintains tense, structure, and meaning.
  • Option 1 (were being instructed) → Past Continuous, incorrect.
  • Option 2 (had been instructed) → Past Perfect, incorrect.
  • Option 3 (are instructed) → Present Tense, incorrect.

question:
Arrange the following sentences to form a coherent paragraph:

P: However, this perceived stability is often an illusion created by lack of data.

Q: Many investors prefer gold because it is traditionally viewed as a safe haven.

R: In reality, its price can be as volatile as any fiat currency under stress.

S: Historical trends suggest that it holds value during periods of high inflation.
option_1: SRPQ
option_2: QSPR
option_3: SQRP
option_4: QPSR
solution_text:
Correct Order: Q → S → P → R

Explanation:

  • Q introduces the topic: investors prefer gold as a safe haven.
  • S logically supports this by giving a reason (historical trends show it holds value during inflation).
  • P then contrasts this belief using “However”, indicating that this stability may be an illusion.
  • R concludes the idea by stating the reality — gold can be volatile like fiat currency.

Final Paragraph:
Many investors prefer gold because it is traditionally viewed as a safe haven. Historical trends suggest that it holds value during periods of high inflation. However, this perceived stability is often an illusion created by lack of data. In reality, its price can be as volatile as any fiat currency under stress.
